Aurivis Energies SE — Operational & Data Protection Incident Report, 14 May 2026; issuer admitted to trading on the FictaReg Market (EU regulated market).
Summary: transient outage in ESG data lake and misrouted vendor SFTP connections led to attempted access to HR onboarding forms and CSRD metrics; no sustained system compromise.
Data categories implicated: employee identifiers, contact data, and payroll IBAN placeholders; no special category data under GDPR Art. 9 was processed in the affected flow.
Privacy workflow events: (i) cross‑border transfer request for pseudonymised training dataset to processor Novalink Systems S.A. in Galenia (non‑adequate third country) approved under 2021 SCCs with TIAs, (ii) DSAR received from Mr. Anders Vilhelm (Art. 15) and erasure request upheld for legacy badge logs (Art. 17).
Consent withdrawal: Ms. Petra Novák withdrew consent for direct marketing on 12 May; CRM suppression list updated and retention shortened to 6 months for related records.
Minimisation and retention: onboarding forms revised to remove national ID scan fields, and a 24‑month retention cap reaffirmed in the DPA Schedule 2 with Novalink.
Data‑export approval ID AEG‑DX‑0441 recorded by the Export Council; transfer delayed until encryption‑at‑rest keys rotated and EU‑only back‑ups verified.
Incident containment: misrouted SFTP keys revoked within 90 minutes; no evidence of exfiltration, access logs show only internal service account queries, and no notification to the supervisory authority was required under GDPR Art. 33.
MAR workflow: preliminary, non‑public Q2 yield shortfall at the Nordholm wind cluster identified as inside information; disclosure delay under MAR Art. 17(4) documented, insider list updated, and the competent authority notified post‑remediation.
We confirm no investor‑facing guidance has been changed and no external dissemination of inside information occurred.
CSRD/SFDR impact assessment: temporary unavailability of Scope 2 data feeds may affect KPI publication timetables but does not alter taxonomy‑eligibility calculations; fallback methodology note prepared.
Security measures applied: network segmentation tightened, vendor SFTP paths whitelisted, and data at rest re‑encrypted; special category data processing remains prohibited for this system per policy and audit confirms compliance.
Controller‑Processor terms: DPA Schedule 1 (processing instructions) appended, international transfer addendum executed with SCC Module 2, and sub‑processor Novalink’s audit report annexed.
Negative assurance: no biometric templates, geolocation, or children’s data are stored in the ESG lake; legacy archive containing sick‑leave notes was fully purged in March under an approved erasure ticket.
Business continuity: trading operations unaffected; internal MAR wall‑crossing procedures were rehearsed but not triggered beyond the core finance team.
Contact: Dr. Irena Salcedo, Data Protection Officer, dpo@aurivis.example; market queries to compliance@aurivis.example.
